title: Doc-Init Rec#3 — Stop hook template + settings.json merge-insert
description: Create templates/claude-hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs implementing sentinel-based logic: if .fabric/forensic.json exists AND .fabric/init-context.json is missing, return {decision:'block', reason:'...use agents-md-init skill...'}; else exit 0. Update init.ts to copy the hook script to target .claude/hooks/ and perform a merge-insert into target .claude/settings.json adding the Stop hook entry (non-destructive: skip if matcher already present). As a side benefit, also add agents-md-reminder.cjs template for future use.

files.target: Claude Code Stop hook sentinel script
files.change: CommonJS script: read hook input from stdin (JSON); check existence of .fabric/forensic.json and .fabric/init-context.json relative to cwd; if forensic exists AND init-context missing: output JSON {decision:'block', reason: '<message telling user to run agents-md-init skill>'}; otherwise: process.exit(0)

files.target: Claude Code Stop hook for ongoing agents-md maintenance (side benefit)
files.change: CommonJS script (side benefit): sentinel for agents-md maintenance workflow — stub implementation as specified in 思路.md; outputs block+reason if AGENTS.md stale conditions detected

files.target: initFabric() — hook copy + settings.json merge-insert steps
files.change: After SKILL.md copy: (1) copy agents-md-init-reminder.cjs to target .claude/hooks/; non-destructive (skip if exists); (2) merge-insert target .claude/settings.json: load existing JSON or create empty {}, locate hooks.Stop array, check if matcher 'agents-md-init-reminder' already present, if not push new Stop hook entry {matcher: '*', hooks: [{type:'command', command:'.claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs'}]}; write back atomically

implementation: Create templates/claude-hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs: read process.stdin as JSON (Claude Code passes hook context); resolve paths relative to process.cwd(); fs.existsSync checks for .fabric/forensic.json and .fabric/init-context.json; conditional JSON output to stdout (decision:block with reason) or process.exit(0)
Create templates/claude-hooks/agents-md-reminder.cjs as a minimal stub (side benefit) following same pattern from 思路.md specification

Update init.ts: add hook copy step with existsSync non-destructive guard; implement mergeInsertStopHook(targetDir) helper: load .claude/settings.json (or {}), check hooks.Stop array for existing agents-md-init-reminder matcher, push if absent, write file atomically; handle case where settings.json has other Stop hooks without disturbing them

reference.examples: bootstrap.ts merge pattern; 思路.md lines 140-207 for hook block+reason JSON format and settings.json structure

criteria: Running fab init on a fresh target: .claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs is created and executable
Running .claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs with .fabric/forensic.json present and .fabric/init-context.json absent: stdout contains {decision:'block'} JSON
Running .claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs with both forensic.json and init-context.json present: exits 0 with no output
fab init merge-inserts .claude/settings.json: hooks.Stop array contains agents-md-init-reminder entry
fab init on target with existing .claude/settings.json containing other Stop hooks: other hooks are preserved (non-destructive merge)
fab init a second time: hook file and settings.json entry both skipped (idempotent)

rationale.chosen_approach: Sentinel-based Stop hook covers cross-session and external-terminal fab init scenarios; merge-insert preserves existing user settings
alternatives_considered: Only stdout reason text (unreliable for cross-session scenarios)
Replace entire settings.json (destructive, would remove user's other hooks)
decision_factors: Dual-trigger reliability: stdout covers same-session, Stop hook covers cross-session
Non-destructive merge: user's existing Claude Code settings must not be disturbed
Sentinel pattern avoids time-based expiry issues
rationale.tradeoffs: Stop hook fires on every Claude Code session stop until init-context.json exists — desired behavior but slightly noisy until user completes initialization

manual_checks: fab init on fresh target — verify .claude/settings.json has Stop hooks entry for agents-md-init-reminder
Manually run: echo '{}' | node .claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs — with forensic present + init-context absent: should output {decision:'block',...}
Manually run same hook with both files present — should exit 0 silently
fab init on target with existing settings.json containing arbitrary hooks — verify those hooks survive intact
success_metrics: Hook returns {decision:'block'} in forensic-present + init-context-absent scenario
settings.json merge is idempotent: running init twice does not duplicate the hook entry
Existing settings.json entries are preserved after merge
unit: init-claude-install.test.ts (TASK-010): assert .claude/hooks/agents-md-init-reminder.cjs created; assert .claude/settings.json Stop hook entry present
integration: init-nondestructive.test.ts (TASK-010): second fab init run skips hook and settings entry

risks.description: settings.json JSON parsing failure if user has comments or trailing commas
risks.mitigation: Use a lenient JSON parser (JSON5 or strip-json-comments) or validate before parsing; emit warning and skip merge if unparseable
risks.fallback: Skip settings.json merge, emit stderr warning — user can manually add the hook
